documentation.description:
-
   Simplified analogue GPIO using Pulse Width Modulation (PWM).

   Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) allows us to generate analogue-like outputs
   on digital GPIO pins by varying the duty cycle of a high-frequency square
   wave. This package provides a simple, reusable interface for PWM-based
   analogue output on the Raspberry Pi Pico. It is designed to be a universal
   library component, saving us from repetitive code and helping the community.

simple_types.name: Percentage
simple_types.code: type Percentage is delta 0.1 digits 4 range 0.0 .. 100.0;

subprograms.code:
function "*"
   (Left  : in RP.PWM.Period;
    Right : in Analog_Level)
    return RP.PWM.Period

function "*"
   (Left  : in RP.PWM.Period;
    Right : in Percentage)
    return RP.PWM.Period

subprograms.name: Get_Base
subprograms.code: function Get_Base (Point : in PWM_Point) return RP.PWM.PWM_Point

subprograms.name: Get_Frequency
subprograms.code: function Get_Frequency (Point : in PWM_Point) return RP.Hertz

subprograms.name: Get_Reload
subprograms.code: function Get_Reload (Point : in PWM_Point) return RP.PWM.Period

subprograms.name: Map_Analog_Level
Map_Percentage
To_PWM
Write_Analog
subprograms.code: procedure Write_Analog (Point : in PWM_Point; Level : in Analog_Level)

subprograms.name: Write_Analog
subprograms.code: procedure Write_Analog (Point : in PWM_Point; Level : in Percentage)
